question: Which validation mode does useForm use by default?

options.explanation: onChange triggers validation on every field change, which can feel intrusive to the user
onBlur triggers validation when a field loses focus
all triggers validation both on change and on blur

question: How does watch differ from getValues?

options.explanation: Speed is not the difference — the distinction is in subscribing to changes

options.text: watch triggers a re-render when the value changes; getValues does not
getValues only works with registered fields
options.explanation: Both methods work with registered fields
watch subscribes to changes and triggers re-renders; getValues simply reads the current value

question: Why should formState be destructured immediately when retrieved from useForm?

options.explanation: TypeScript does not require destructuring — this is related to RHF's Proxy mechanism

options.text: formState is a Proxy, and destructuring ensures correct subscription to changes
Without destructuring, formState will be undefined
options.explanation: formState will not be undefined — but the Proxy won't be able to track which properties you're accessing

options.text: It is just a code style convention with no technical reason
options.explanation: This is a technical requirement: the Proxy tracks property access to optimize re-renders

question: Which of these methods/properties does useForm return?

options.explanation: useFieldArray is a separate hook imported from react-hook-form, not a property of useForm

question: What happens if you use <input type="number"> without valueAsNumber: true?

options.text: There will be a TypeScript compilation error
options.explanation: TypeScript will not throw an error — the value will simply be a string instead of a number

options.text: The value will be a string, not a number

options.explanation: The field will work, but the value will be of type string

options.text: RHF will automatically convert the value to a number
options.explanation: RHF does not convert automatically — you must explicitly set valueAsNumber: true
